journal_entry.description: Revenue recognition for services delivered against customer deposit
journal_entry.rationale: Customer previously provided a $100 deposit which was recorded as a liability (Unearned Revenue or Customer Deposits). Now that the service has been delivered, we recognize the revenue by decreasing the liability (debit) and increasing revenue (credit). The debit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) indicates one liability decrease line (slot d), and the credit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) indicates one revenue increase line (slot c). No tax lines are added because the HST/GST would have been collected and recorded at the time of the original deposit. This transaction is merely the revenue recognition event.

output.reason: When a service is delivered against a customer deposit, we recognize revenue. The credit side records: (1) Revenue increase from service delivery = revenue increase. The debit side (not classified here) would decrease the liability (customer deposit/unearned revenue).

credit_classifier.reason: When a service is delivered against a customer deposit, we recognize revenue. The credit side records: (1) Revenue increase from service delivery = revenue increase. The debit side (not classified here) would decrease the liability (customer deposit/unearned revenue).

output.reason: When a customer deposit (unearned revenue liability) is earned by delivering the service, the liability must be decreased. Debit: Liability decrease (customer deposit/unearned revenue). Credit side (not classified here) would be revenue increase.

debit_classifier.reason: When a customer deposit (unearned revenue liability) is earned by delivering the service, the liability must be decreased. Debit: Liability decrease (customer deposit/unearned revenue). Credit side (not classified here) would be revenue increase.

output.description: Revenue recognition for services delivered against customer deposit
output.rationale: Customer previously provided a $100 deposit which was recorded as a liability (Unearned Revenue or Customer Deposits). Now that the service has been delivered, we recognize the revenue by decreasing the liability (debit) and increasing revenue (credit). The debit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) indicates one liability decrease line (slot d), and the credit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) indicates one revenue increase line (slot c). No tax lines are added because the HST/GST would have been collected and recorded at the time of the original deposit. This transaction is merely the revenue recognition event.

output.reason: Entry correctly records revenue recognition for services delivered against customer deposit. Balance verified: $100 debit = $100 credit. Unearned Revenue (liability) correctly decreased with debit, Service Revenue correctly increased with credit. Amount matches transaction ($100). No tax lines needed as tax would have been handled at time of original deposit receipt. All accounts appropriate and directionally correct per IFRS revenue recognition principles.
